notes: FROZEN (daemon=incapable, route=frozen) — no phases, per the SKILL frozen rule. The CORRECT spec (asserted here, never weakened to the daemon limit): user-esc-interrupt is ready → working → ready. A >2s prompt drives ready → working; the user presses ESC mid-turn; state MUST settle back to ready (must not stick in working); a follow-up prompt drives a clean ready → working → ready second turn proving the parser reset. kiro-cli CANNOT be observed for this today: the daemon tails the .jsonl Source whose event vocabulary (Prompt/AssistantMessage/ToolResults/Clear) carries no cancellation kind, and the kiro parser sets no IsUserInterrupt — so classifier rule 3 (the working→ready settle on ESC) can never fire and the session sticks in working. The cancellation IS recorded by kiro, but only in the untailed <uuid>.json sidecar (session_state.conversation_metadata.user_turn_metadatas[].end_reason == Cancelled). Unfreeze = (daemon) a sidecar end_reason reader feeding the classifier + (driver) port the interrupt step; then re-assess to yes/full/ready, add the ready→working→ready phases, and record. The .jsonl carries no model/token/cost, so observations assert tokens_nonzero=false, cost_nonzero=false, model unknown.
